name: Unitree Robotics SDK
what_it_does: C++/Python SDK and REST-like API for controlling Unitree quadruped and humanoid robots (Go2, H1, B2, G1) including motion control, sensor access, and custom locomotion policies.
use_cases: Controlling quadruped robot (Go2/B2) locomotion and custom gaits from agent code
Accessing lidar, IMU, and camera sensor data streams for perception tasks
Deploying custom RL locomotion policies on Unitree hardware
Automating inspection and patrol routes with programmatic waypoint navigation
Integrating humanoid robot (H1/G1) with agent-orchestrated manipulation tasks
not_for: Pure simulation without hardware (use Isaac Sim or Mujoco instead)
High-level task planning without ROS2 or robotics expertise
best_when: An agent needs to command Unitree robot hardware for physical world tasks like inspection, delivery, or demonstration.
avoid_when: You need a simulator-first development workflow or cross-platform robot abstraction.

auth.notes: SDK connects over local network (WiFi/Ethernet) to robot's onboard computer. No cloud auth required. Physical network access to the robot is the security boundary.

pricing.free_tier_limits: SDK is free and open-source; requires purchasing Unitree hardware ($16k-$90k+)
pricing.paid_tiers: Go2 EDU ~$16k, B2 ~$40k, H1 ~$90k, G1 ~$16k (prices approximate)
pricing.notes: The SDK itself is open-source (BSD). The barrier is hardware cost, not software licensing.

performance.rate_limits: Real-time control loop typically 100-500Hz; sensor streams at hardware refresh rates

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation is sparse and primarily in Chinese with partial English translations. Error handling relies heavily on hardware state codes that require domain expertise to interpret.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Real-time control commands are not idempotent - each command directly moves the robot. Idempotency must be managed at the application level.
known_agent_gotchas: Commands sent out of safety bounds may trigger emergency stop requiring manual reset
Documentation is primarily in Chinese; English docs are translations of varying quality
SDK version compatibility with robot firmware must be matched carefully
State machine must be in correct mode before issuing motion commands
Physical safety zone management is entirely the developer's responsibility

security_score_components.security_notes: Local network communication with no TLS or authentication by default. Physical network segmentation is the primary security control. Not suitable for internet-facing deployments.
